title: `quiver:share` Command
objective: Ship `npx create-quiver share <slice>` that wraps `quiver:replay` into a tar.gz bundle suitable for handoff to another agent or user without the full repository clone.
description: When a handoff needs more than HANDOFF.md (for example when another agent needs to see the actual source files declared in the slice), copying each file by hand is error-prone. `quiver:share` produces a self-contained bundle in tar.gz that the recipient can unpack and read. It optionally strips common secret patterns (`.env`, API keys) before packaging.
must: Internally call `replay` to produce the bundle in a temp directory
Package the directory into `<slice-id>.tar.gz` using Node streams (no external tar binary)
Support `--strip-env` to redact lines matching common secret patterns (`/^[A-Z_]+=.*/`, `sk-*`, `ghp_*`) with `<REDACTED>`
Support `--out <path>` for the output archive location
Print the archive path and a short checksum on completion
Add a `docs/COMMANDS.md` row marked `experimental` and update `README.md` npm scripts list
not_included: Uploading the archive anywhere (email, gist, cloud)
Exhaustive secret detection (users still review before sharing)
acceptance: `npx create-quiver share <slice>` produces a `.tar.gz` in the current directory
`tar -tzf <archive>` lists at least AGENTS.md, QUICK.md, PROJECT_MAP.md, and the slice's files
`--strip-env` redacts lines that match common secret patterns in bundled files
The archive unpacks on macOS, Linux, and Windows (tested in CI)
assumptions: Node's `zlib` and `tar` modules (or `tar` npm package) are sufficient; no external `tar` binary required
Users understand that `--strip-env` is best-effort and still review before sending
The bundle format is informal; no strict schema beyond the replay contract
blocked_reason: Deferred: needs ≥1 real handoff where HANDOFF.md alone was insufficient
